## [03:01 - 06:02]

So that's why retargeting that's where retargeting stacks come in place where you essentially break down in this example we're using people that visited a website you break down the people that visited your website and instead of showing everyone who visited your website in the last 180 days the same retargeting ad. You break it down by people who visited in the last 10 days people who visited in the last 20 days people who visited in the last 30 days. And each one of those is run as its own targeting which means its own ad set which means you can have its own creative right so they're seeing a different thing based on when they triggered that event. Instead of just retargeting people with the same creative for almost half a year which is not a great way to use your money.
